snippets.title: Indexing, slicing and immutability
snippets.code:
s = "Hello, world"
print(s[0], s[-1])      # 'H' 'd'
print(s[7:12])           # 'world'
print(s.upper())         # 'HELLO, WORLD'  -> NEW string

# Immutability:
# s[0] = 'h'             # TypeError
s = 'h' + s[1:]          # produce a new string instead
print(s)                 # 'hello, world'
snippets.explanation: Methods like `upper`, `replace`, slicing — all return new strings.

snippets.title: Build with join, not +=
snippets.code:
parts = []
for token in stream():
    parts.append(token)
text = "".join(parts)   # O(n)

# Don't do this (O(n^2)):
# text = ""
# for token in stream():
#     text = text + token
snippets.explanation: join allocates once and copies once; += allocates and copies for every step.

snippets.title: Format with f-strings
snippets.code:
model = "gpt-4o-mini"
price = 0.000150
print(f"{model}: ${price:.4f} per 1K tokens")

# Multi-line, with expressions:
prompt = (
    f"You are a helpful assistant.\n"
    f"Use up to {max_tokens} tokens."
)
snippets.explanation: f-strings are the modern way to format; they support format-specs and expressions inline.

snippets.title: Strings vs bytes
snippets.code:
s = "héllo, 🌍"
b = s.encode("utf-8")        # bytes:  b'h\xc3\xa9llo, \xf0\x9f\x8c\x8d'
back = b.decode("utf-8")     # 'héllo, 🌍'

len(s), len(b)                # (8, 13)  - characters vs UTF-8 bytes
# s + b                       # TypeError - cannot mix str and bytes
snippets.explanation: Always encode/decode at boundaries (files, sockets); never mix str and bytes mid-flow.

snippets.title: Splitting and joining cleanly
snippets.code:
csv_line = "id, title, score"
cols = [c.strip() for c in csv_line.split(",")]
print(cols)               # ['id', 'title', 'score']

rebuilt = ",".join(cols)
print(rebuilt)            # 'id,title,score'
snippets.explanation: `split` + comprehension + `join` is the canonical text-pipeline trio.
